summary: Validated inline (non-modal) Compose & Reply email editors on /mail and the new CRM Files & Documents attach flow. All test-ids present and functional. No modal/dialog wraps the compose or reply composer. CRM document picker opens as a modal dialog (acceptable per spec), shows expected categories (Company Certifications, Proposals, Sales Materials), the seeded 'CRM Attach Test Doc' is listed, can be selected/attached, and the chip renders in mail-composer-attachments. Reply renders inline at the bottom of the conversation with read-only To and Cancel hides it. Send button was NOT clicked, no real emails were dispatched.

issues: Console warning on every composer mount: 'quill Cannot register "bullet" specified in "formats" config. Are you sure it was registered?' — non-blocking but spams console; remove 'bullet' from formats or import the Quill bullet/list module explicitly.

action_items: Optional cleanup: fix Quill 'bullet' format warning in RichEmailEditor to remove repeated console errors when composer mounts.
critical_code_review_comments: InlineComposer correctly toggles toEditable for reply (read-only recipient) and shares the same component for compose/reply/reply-all – good DRY.
CrmDocumentPicker uses 'all' sentinel for category/subcategory and disables subcategory until a real category is chosen – matches Files & Documents page behavior.
Already-selected docs in the picker are disabled to prevent duplicate attachments – good UX.
Quill bullet format warning is a real (non-fatal) misconfiguration in RichEmailEditor that should be cleaned up.
seed_data_creation: None — used the pre-existing 'CRM Attach Test Doc' seeded by the main agent.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Inline compose/reply UI + CRM doc picker flow already validated end-to-end (without clicking Send). The Quill 'bullet' console warning is pre-existing and unrelated to this feature. If retesting, login → /mail → click data-testid='mail-compose-btn' → verify mail-composer is NOT wrapped in role='dialog' → click mail-composer-attach-crm → search 'CRM Attach' → select → click doc-picker-confirm → verify mail-composer-attachments chip. For reply, open any mail-item-*, click reply-btn-*, verify mail-reply-panel/mail-reply-composer present and not inside a dialog.
